audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Spirit-led wilderness testing, Greek testing/adversary language, scripture replies, kingdoms temptation, messenger service, Galilee light citation, metanoeite/basileia proclamation, first disciples, healing summary.

verses.restored: Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tested by the adversarial one.
After fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry.
The tester came and said to him, If you are Son of the Divine, speak so that these stones may become loaves of bread.
But he answered, It has been written: A human shall not live by bread alone, but by every word going out through the mouth of the Divine.
Then the adversarial one took him into the holy city and stood him on the pinnacle of the temple.

He said to him, If you are Son of the Divine, throw yourself down, for it has been written: He will command his messengers concerning you, and on their hands they will lift you up, lest you strike your foot against a stone.
Jesus said to him, Again it has been written: You shall not test the Lord your God.
Again, the adversarial one took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the realms of the world and their glory.
He said to him, All these I will give you, if you fall down and bow/prostrate before me.
Then Jesus said to him, Go away, adversary, for it has been written: You shall bow/prostrate before the Lord your God, and him alone you shall serve.
Then the adversarial one left him, and look, messengers came and served him.
When Jesus heard that John had been handed over, he withdrew into Galilee.
Leaving Nazareth, he came and dwelled in Capernaum by the sea, in the territories of Zebulun and Naphtali,
so that what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled, saying:
Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, way of the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the nations,
the people sitting in darkness saw a great light, and for those sitting in the region and shadow of death, light rose for them.
From then Jesus began to proclaim and say, Turn and realign your mind/heart, for the reign of the heavens has drawn near.
Walking beside the Sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers, Simon called Peter and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea, for they were fishers.
He said to them, Come after me, and I will make you fishers of people.
Immediately they left the nets and followed him.
Going on from there, he saw two other brothers, James son of Zebedee and John his brother, in the boat with Zebedee their father, mending their nets, and he called them.
Immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
Jesus went around all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, proclaiming the good message of the reign, and healing every disease and every weakness among the people.

The report about him went out into all Syria, and they brought to him all who were sick, held by various diseases and torments, those troubled by unclean spirits, those with seizures, and paralyzed people, and he healed them.
Great crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and beyond the Jordan.
